Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi to Salalah by Road: Bus Time and Ticket Fare Review

August 14, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Traveling from Abu Dhabi to Salalah by bus offers an opportunity to experience the diverse landscapes between the UAE and Oman.

Overview of Bus Travel: Duration, Cost, and Distance

The journey by bus from Abu Dhabi to Salalah is a lengthy but comfortable experience, spanning approximately 21 hours and 10 minutes. This extended travel time allows passengers to appreciate the changing scenery along the route. The cost of a bus ticket ranges from AED 128 to AED 191, making it an affordable choice for those looking to explore Oman. The distance between Abu Dhabi and Salalah is around 832.0 km, with a road distance of approximately 1244.5 km.

Also See: Sharjah to Salalah by Road: Bus Schedule and Fare Details

Bus Operators and Routes

1. Mwasalat

  • Phone: 009681551

  • Email: info@mwasalat. om
  • Website: mwasalat. om
  • Routes: Abu Dhabi to Al-Buraimi: Operates once daily with an average duration of 3 hours 2 minutes. Ticket price: AED 110160. Buraimi to Burj Sahwa: Operates four times a day with an average duration of 4 hours 3 minutes. Ticket price: AED 150210. -item

Burj Sahwa to Salalah: Operates twice daily with an average duration of 12 hours 4 minutes. Ticket price: AED 300430.

2. Salalah Line Express

  • Phone: 91263098

  • Email: salalahlineexpress@gmail. com
  • Route: From Burj Sahwa Bus Station to Commercial Center Street, operating twice daily with an average duration of 11 hours.

3. Dubai RTA Bus

  • Phone: 8009090

  • Email: ask@rta. ae
  • Website: rta. ae
  • Routes: -item
Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

Abu Dhabi Central Bus Station to Max Metro Bus Stop Landside 1: Duration 2 hours 5 minutes. Frequency: Every 30 minutes. Estimated fare: AED 110160.

Catching the Bus

  • From Abu Dhabi: Bus services from Abu Dhabi to Salalah, operated by Mwasalat, depart from Abu Dhabi Central Bus Station.

  • Arrival in Salalah: The buses arrive at Al-Buraimi Bus Station in Salalah.

Accommodation Near Salalah

Upon arrival in Salalah, travelers have a wide range of accommodation options to choose from. With over 308 hotels available, prices start at AED 21 per night, providing various options to suit different preferences and budgets. Many of these hotels offer easy access to local attractions and amenities, enhancing your stay in Salalah.

Read More: How to Travel from Dubai to Salalah by Public Transport Guide

Additional Tips

  • Check for Updates: Before embarking on your journey, check the latest schedules and travel updates on the Mwasalat website or contact their customer service for any changes or delays.
  • Comfort Essentials: Pack essentials for a long journey, such as water, snacks, a travel pillow, and entertainment like books or music, to make your trip more comfortable.
  • Prepare for Transfers: Since there are no direct buses, be prepared for transfers at various points along the route. Keep your belongings secure and allow extra time for transfers.
  • Travel Documents: Ensure you have all necessary travel documents, including your passport and any required visas, ready for inspection at checkpoints.
  • Local Currency: Carry some Omani rials for any purchases or expenses during your stay in Salalah, as not all places may accept credit cards.

Traveling by bus from Abu Dhabi to Salalah offers a budget-friendly and scenic option for those eager to explore Oman. This comprehensive guide should help ensure your journey is as smooth and enjoyable as possible. Embrace the adventure, take in the stunning views, and enjoy an economical and immersive travel experience.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: August 14, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
